page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-16-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance featuring 16 dedicated virtual CPUs and 64 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a balanced allocation of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance operates without integrated GPU hardware and does not include local storage.

The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities for workloads requiring stable compute resources. Because this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it represents a streamlined option for resource-balanced deployments. The hardware profile offers a standard resource density, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for applications that do not require specialized hardware acceleration or high-speed local disk access.

This server configuration is suitable for general-purpose workloads, including mid-tier web serving, application servers, containerized microservices, and moderate database hosting. It is also appropriate for continuous integration and continuous deployment pipelines that benefit from dedicated multi-core processing without the overhead of specialized storage or graphics hardware.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-16-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB of system memory. This setup establishes a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, providing 4 GB of memory per core. The instance does not feature local storage or GPU accelerators, focusing resources entirely on compute and memory capacity. This streamlined hardware profile is designed for consistent performance without the overhead of specialized components. It is well-suited for general-purpose applications, including containerized microservices, application servers, mid-tier web serving, and CI/CD pipelines. The dedicated vCPU allocation ensures predictable execution times for multi-threaded tasks, making it a reliable option for standard enterprise workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-16-lssd is an x86_64 server featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for balanced, general-purpose enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-16-lssd is an x86_64 server with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB RAM for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 compute instance featuring sixteen dedicated virtual cores and sixty-four gigabytes of memory for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 64 GB of system memory with 4 GB per vCPU
No integrated GPU or local storage configuration
Suitable for web serving, microservices, and CI/CD pipelines
